channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: St. Francis softball hurls combined no-hitter, takes down Whitney to claim Livermore Stampede championship
item.description: St. Francis softball beat Whitney on Saturday night in Livermore, finishing a perfect 6-0 run through the Livermore Stampede. Several other Bay Area teams competed at the three-day showcase in the East Bay.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Connor stars as Jets clinch Central Division title with shootout victory over Blackhawks
item.description: Kyle Connor and Cole Perfetti scored in the shootout, and the Winnipeg Jets clinched the franchise’s first Central Division title and the No. 1 seed for the Western Conference playoffs with a wild 5-4 victory over the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Baldwin's pinch-hit single in 9th lifts White Sox over Red Sox 3-2
item.description: Pinch-hitter Brooks Baldwin lined an RBI single off Boston closer Aroldis Chapman with one out in the bottom of the ninth, lifting the Chicago White Sox to a 3-2 win over the Red Sox on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan snatched the ball out of Mike Trout’s glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands to make what appeared to be a great catch.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gunner Stockton strengthens hold on Georgia starting quarterback job with strong spring practice
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Carson Beck's decision to transfer to Miami opened the door for Gunner Stockton to take over as Georgia's starting quarterback.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Game 82: The end of the line for some in the NBA, decision day for others
item.description: Game 82 for every team in the NBA has arrived. For 10 clubs, it's the end of the season. For 13 others, it's one last game before the postseason. And for seven teams in the Western Conference, Game 82 might feel like Game 7.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What needs to happen for Warriors to reach playoff series … or play-in tournament
item.description: The Warriors could still drop to the play-in tournament if the results don't go their way on final day of regular season.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Paige Spiranac praises Nick Dunlap for finishing out Masters participation despite poor play
item.description: Nick Dunlap received praise on Friday for sticking it out and playing the second round of the Masters despite his awful first round on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Domantas Sabonis set to win 3rd straight NBA rebound title, eyeing another All-NBA spot
item.description: Sacramento's Domantas Sabonis is about to join a pretty special group of rebounders. There are six players in NBA history to win at least three consecutive rebound titles: Wilt Chamberlain, Dennis Rodman, Moses Malone, Kevin Garnett and Andre Drummond.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Landeskog 'excited about what the future looks like' after playing in a game for 1st time in 3 years
item.description: LOVELAND, Colo. (AP) — For the first time in 1,020 or so days, Gabriel Landeskog has some fresh game tape to examine. Game tape of him hitting players, taking hits, being called for a penalty and even putting someone in a headlock.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Phil Wertman Invitational: GJ's Bryce Flanagan dominates 800m and more
item.description: The sun scorched down on Stocker Stadium Friday for the Phil Wertman Invitational. But the heat did not stop many of the Western Slope's top athletes from showing off their speed, power and verticals.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Julian Phillips scores a career-high 23 points and Bulls rout Wizards 119-89
item.description: Julian Phillips scored a career-high 23 points on 7-of-9 shooting, Nikola Vucevic finished with 15 points, 12 rebounds and eight assists and the Chicago Bulls beat the Washington Wizards 119-89 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tickets to the Salt Lake Bees new stadium will cost you more than they used to
item.description: The Salt Lake Bees are playing their first game at the new ballpark in South Jordan tomorrow, though fans of the team might notice that prices are a little different than Smith's Ballpark.
item.dc:creator: Sorina Trauntvein, ABC 4

item.title: Mikheyev, Nazar and Knight lead the Blackhawks to a 3-1 win over the Penguins
item.description: Ilya Mikheyev scored twice, Frank Nazar added a goal and Spencer Knight stopped 28 shots as the Chicago Blackhawks snapped a five-game skid with a 3-1 win over the Pittsburgh Penguins on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
